skill.name: async-python-patterns
skill.description: Guides developers through Python asyncio patterns for concurrent I/O, async APIs, background tasks, streaming workflows, and testing.
skill.summary: Learn practical asyncio patterns for building non-blocking Python applications.
skill.author: ActiveInferenceInstitute

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command detections, but review found they are Markdown code fences and Python asyncio examples, not shell execution. The remaining concerns are educational examples that include outbound HTTP requests and interpolated SQL strings if copied without adaptation. No prompt injection attempt, credential access, obfuscation, persistence, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Python code fences, inline backticks, or asyncio examples. They do not invoke a shell, subprocess, Ruby backtick execution, or system command interface.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are fenced Markdown blocks and Python async snippets. No command execution API or shell syntax is present at these representative static locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The high static finding at line 3 points to YAML description text for the skill. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata describing async Python patterns. It contains no cryptographic API, algorithm name, or secret handling logic.

low_findings.title: Copyable Network Examples May Make Outbound Requests
low_findings.description: The skill includes example URLs and aiohttp request patterns. This is appropriate for async networking education, but users should replace sample hosts and observe permission, rate limit, and privacy requirements.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly include HTTP URLs and request construction. They are not hidden or malicious, but they can cause outbound traffic if copied and run.

low_findings.title: SQL Query Interpolation Appears In Educational Example
low_findings.description: The async database example builds SQL strings with f-string interpolation. The shown input is an integer, but copied patterns should use parameterized queries for untrusted values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The query strings interpolate a variable directly into SQL. The surrounding code is simulated and typed as int, so the issue is a copy-paste guidance risk rather than active exploitation.

content.value_statement: Async Python code can become hard to structure, test, and debug. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for asyncio applications.
actual_capabilities: Explains core asyncio concepts, including coroutines, tasks, futures, and the event loop.
Provides patterns for gather, task creation, timeouts, cancellation, locks, and semaphores.
Shows async context managers, async iterators, and producer-consumer queues.
Demonstrates common application patterns for web scraping, databases, WebSockets, and connection pools.
Covers testing async code with pytest-asyncio and timeout assertions.
limitations: It is a reference guide and does not inspect a repository automatically.
Examples use simulated clients and placeholder URLs that require adaptation.
It does not provide framework-specific production hardening for every async library.
It does not replace load testing or security review of networked applications.

use_cases.title: Design an Async API Service
use_cases.description: Use the patterns to structure non-blocking handlers, background tasks, and timeout handling for an API service.

use_cases.title: Improve Concurrent I/O Workflows
use_cases.description: Apply gather, semaphores, queues, and connection pools to speed up independent network or database operations.

use_cases.title: Review Async Code Quality
use_cases.description: Check async code for missing awaits, event loop blocking, cancellation gaps, and weak test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Async Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how coroutines, tasks, and the event loop work in my Python use case. Keep the explanation practical and include common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Blocking Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert synchronous I/O into async code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Python function and suggest an asyncio version. Identify any blocking calls, missing awaits, and cleanup requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Design Concurrent Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an async producer-consumer workflow for these tasks. Include queue sizing, cancellation behavior, timeout handling, and error reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Async Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this async Python architecture for event loop blocking, unbounded concurrency, resource leaks, cancellation handling, and test gaps.

output_examples.input: I need to fetch many API resources without overwhelming the remote service.
output_examples.output: The response recommends bounded concurrency with a semaphore, per-request timeouts, retry limits, and connection pooling.

output_examples.input: My async tests pass locally but sometimes hang in CI.
output_examples.output: The response identifies likely missing awaits, unhandled cancellation, blocking calls, and tests that need explicit timeout assertions.

output_examples.input: I want to process jobs while keeping the API responsive.
output_examples.output: The response outlines a queue-based design with background tasks, graceful shutdown, error collection, and backpressure.

best_practices: Use bounded concurrency for network, database, and batch workloads.
Add timeouts, cancellation handling, and cleanup paths to long-running tasks.
Test async behavior with realistic failures, slow operations, and resource cleanup.
anti_patterns: Do not call blocking I/O or time.sleep inside async functions.
Do not create unbounded tasks without backpressure or cancellation control.
Do not copy placeholder SQL or network examples into production without validation.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps design and review asyncio-based Python applications that use concurrent I/O and non-blocking workflows.

faq.question: Does it require a specific web framework?
faq.answer: No. It references common async libraries, but the patterns apply across many Python frameworks.

faq.question: Can it help with performance problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on I/O-bound performance issues such as unbounded concurrency, blocking calls, and missing connection pools.

faq.question: Can it generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: It can guide production code, but examples still need project-specific validation, security review, and testing.

faq.question: Does it cover async testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes pytest-asyncio examples and guidance for timeout and failure behavior.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples with low copy-paste risk, but no malicious behavior or prompt injection.
